title: Is there a way to capture the trigger "item updated"?
excerpt:
Hi dear Monday community :slight_smile: 
I’m looking for a solution to capture the trigger “item updated”. 
Not the item attributes but the item title itself. 
This to include that as a trigger in an automation like “if …

posts.author: ECH (Emmanuel CHERIGIE)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi dear Monday community

I’m looking for a solution to capture the trigger “item updated”.
Not the item attributes but the item title itself.

This to include that as a trigger in an automation like “if item title updated then …”

Does it exist?

Thanks in advance for your support!

NB: I cannot go use marketplace as blocked by my company for security reasons.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: Which product (CRM/Work Management, etc) and level (Pro/Enterprise) do you have?

posts.author: ECH (Emmanuel CHERIGIE)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Desiree! Product is “Work management”. For the level, where could I see it please? I’m admin of my workplace but we need to through “super admin” team (at company level) to create them.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: At the top of your screen, do you see this icon?

posts.author: ECH (Emmanuel CHERIGIE)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes I see it:

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
Perfect, that means you likely have Enterprise. That’s the workflow center. You can set up a workflow of ‘when column changes’ and choose the ‘name’ as the column to look for.

image1131×633 36.8 KB

posts.author: ECH (Emmanuel CHERIGIE)
posts.cookedHtml: Wow sounds perfect! I’ll try it right now, thanks!!

posts.author: ECH (Emmanuel CHERIGIE)
posts.cookedHtml: I confirm this is exactly what I was looking for! Thanks a lot Desiree!

posts.author: shadab (Shadab)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey I think the change name event should work for this
